top_stories.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
top_stories.summary: A debug flag left enabled in production builds of multiple Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled token-sharing restrictions, allowing any app on the same device to silently steal account tokens without authentication. The flaw exposed OAuth/access tokens that could be used to read email, access files, browse calendars, and send messages as the authenticated user with no additional prompts or credentials required.

top_stories.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
top_stories.summary: A coding error in Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled a security setting designed to protect authentication, exposing accounts to potential takeover. The flaw affected apps including Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, allowing attackers to steal login credentials and data.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra pushes passkeys, tightens identity security
top_stories.summary: Microsoft has released multiple new identity and network access capabilities for its Entra product family over the past 30 days, with several features reaching general availability. The updates include passkey support and tightened identity security controls aimed at helping organizations implement zero trust security strategies.

top_stories.title: Hardening Microsoft 365 Against Token Theft
top_stories.summary: The article addresses token theft attacks targeting Microsoft 365 environments and methods to harden defenses against them. Key focus areas likely include authentication token security, session management, and protective configurations within Microsoft's identity and access ecosystem.

top_stories.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
top_stories.summary: The Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform has expanded its targeting beyond Microsoft 365 to include AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms. The platform employs device code phishing techniques and has been flagged by the FBI.

news.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
news.summary: Security researchers disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft VS Code that allows attackers to steal GitHub OAuth tokens with read/write repository access. The attack exploits GitHub.dev functionality and can be triggered simply by clicking a malicious link.

news.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
news.summary: The article addresses the concept of "Identity Dark Matter" in enterprise IAM environments, where identity activity occurs outside centralized visibility and control. It focuses on Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP) as a means to reduce attack surface across fragmented identities including machine identities and decentralized systems.

news.title: Unpatched Windows Search URI Vulnerability Lets Attackers Steal NTLMv2 Hashes
news.summary: Researchers at Huntress disclosed an unpatched Windows Search URI handler vulnerability that can be exploited to steal NTLMv2 credential hashes from users. The flaw is related to a similar issue (CVE-2026-33829) affecting the Windows Snipping Tool's URI handler, and no patch is currently available.

news.title: Understanding Microsoft Entra ID Tenant Object Quota
news.summary: The article explains the object quota limits within Microsoft Entra ID tenants, covering what the quota applies to and how administrators can manage it. This is a technical explainer focused on a specific operational constraint within Microsoft's identity platform.

news.title: Amazon OpenSearch Service: Mechanisms to secure your domain
news.summary: Amazon published an overview of security mechanisms available for Amazon OpenSearch Service, covering authentication, authorization, encryption, and network access controls. The post details fine-grained access control implementation and AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) integration for securing OpenSearch domains.

news.title: Keeper Security Modernizes Identity Security for DrillDocs' Global Offshore Operations
news.summary: Keeper Security has deployed its KeeperPAM solution at DrillDocs, an energy technology company managing offshore operations globally. The implementation addresses credential security and privileged access management across a distributed, bring-your-own-device workforce requiring 24/7 engineering workflows.

news.title: Azure Managed Identity Explained with Real-World Examples
news.summary: This article explains Azure Managed Identity, a feature that enables applications to authenticate to Azure resources without storing credentials such as passwords, secrets, or connection strings. The content covers how managed identities work in real-world scenarios, relevant to IAM professionals managing authentication and secrets elimination in cloud environments.

news.title: Preinstall to persistence: Inside the Red Hat npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign
news.summary: A supply chain attack compromised over 90 versions of Red Hat npm packages (@redhat-cloud-services), embedding malicious code in CI/CD environments and developer systems. The attack targets credential theft from GitHub, cloud platforms, and local machines, directly impacting identity and access security in developer workflows.

news.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
news.summary: Dashlane disclosed a brute-force attack on May 31, 2026, in which an external threat actor successfully bypassed two-factor authentication on a small number of accounts, resulting in encrypted vaults being downloaded for fewer than 20 personal subscription users. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in 2FA implementation within password management services, a tool widely used in enterprise IAM environments.

news.title: UPDATE: BIO-key Delivers Phishing-Resistant Biometric Security to Alabama’s AOD Federal Credit Union
news.summary: BIO-key has deployed phishing-resistant biometric authentication at Alabama's AOD Federal Credit Union through a partnership with BlueAlly. The implementation provides biometric security as a phishing-resistant authentication method for the credit union's users.

news.title: New Data: Fractured Tech Stack Slows Down IT
news.summary: JumpCloud released a global research report examining how tool sprawl and mixed device fleets impact IT productivity. The report includes findings relevant to IAM professionals as JumpCloud is a directory and identity management platform vendor.

news.title: Yubico YubiKey 5C NFC
news.summary: Yubico's YubiKey 5C NFC hardware security key is reviewed as an MFA authentication device priced at $58. The device supports multiple authentication protocols and connects via USB-C with NFC capability for use as a second factor in account authentication.

news.title: Identity Elevated: A New Unified Identity Experience in Cisco Cloud Control
news.summary: Cisco has launched a unified identity experience called "Identity in Cloud Control" within its Cisco Cloud Control platform. The solution provides visibility and management capabilities across human, non-human, and AI agent identities, and includes identity-driven agentic operations via an AI Canvas feature.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to signal potential criminal charges. The incident involves Microsoft's response to unsanctioned vulnerability disclosures rather than a coordinated disclosure process.

news.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
news.summary: A Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN authentication bypass vulnerability is under active exploitation across two attack waves beginning in mid-May. The flaw requires specific conditions to exploit but has progressed to active adversarial use, necessitating immediate patching.

news.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
news.summary: A supply chain attack campaign called "Miasma" has compromised Red Hat npm packages (@redhat-cloud-services) to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines. The attack uses install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and deploys a self-propagating worm.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including high-profile accounts belonging to the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions circulating on Telegram demonstrated how to socially engineer the AI bot into bypassing normal account recovery and authentication controls.

news.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
news.summary: Researchers discovered an exploit chain in a popular automation service that combined over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities to enable potential major compromise. The attack chain highlights risks associated with excessive permissions and non-human identity (NHI) management in complex cloud integrations.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers while CISA works to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed credentials for highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.
